title: PM Modi congratulates Balendra Shah on taking oath as Nepal Prime Minister
author: ET Online
long_desc: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday congratulated Balendra Shah on being sworn in as Nepal’s 47th Prime Minister, expressing optimism about strengthening ties between the two countries.In a post on X, PM Modi said, “Warm congratulations to Mr. Balendra Shah on being sworn in as the Prime Minister of Nepal. I look forward to working closely with you to take India-Nepal friendship and cooperation to even greater heights for the mutual benefit of our two peoples.”Rapper-turned-politician Shah was sworn in as Nepal PM on Friday, tasked with restoring political stability and creating jobs in the poor Himalayan nation long troubled by fragile governments and weak growth prospects. Shah became prime minister after his three-year-old Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) won 182 seats in the 275-member parliament in the March 5 election, the first vote after the anti-corruption Gen Z protests in which 76 people were killed in September last year.

title: Centre reduced excise duty on petrol, diesel keeping polls in mind, alleges D K Shivakumar
author: PTI
short_desc: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister D K Shivakumar has stated the central government's decision to reduce excise duty on petrol and diesel is motivated by upcoming elections. He mentioned the Nayara company has already increased prices. Shivakumar indicated the state government will discuss potential me
long_desc: Bengaluru: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister D K Shivakumar said on Friday that the central government's decision to cut excise duty on petrol and diesel was keeping upcoming elections in mind.The government has slashed excise duty on petrol and diesel by Rs 10 per litre each, a move aimed at shielding domestic consumers from a surge in global oil prices triggered by the Middle East conflict, at an estimated revenue cost of Rs 1.75 lakh crore.Also read: Retail petrol, diesel prices won't change after Rs 10/litre tax cut, government saysAlongside, the government brought back duties on export of diesel and aviation turbine fuel (ATF). The Government of India is murdering us...they are doing it for the election. The Nayara company has already increased the prices. We will throw light on this issue to the people of the state,  Shivakumar told reporters here in response to a question.Asked if the state government will take any measures to bring down the prices, he said,  We will talk about it later.  The 2026 Assembly elections are set to take place across four states -- Assam, Keralam, Tamil Nadu and West Bengal, and one Union Territory, Puducherry. There are also bypolls in several states, including for two assembly constituencies in Karnataka.

title: Sunlight illuminating Ram Lalla’s forehead in Ayodhya, PM Modi witnesses 'Surya Tilak'
author: ET Online
long_desc: Ayodhya witnessed grand celebrations on the occasion of Ram Navami, with devotion reaching its peak during the much-awaited Surya Tilak ceremony at the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Temple. At exactly 12 noon, a precisely aligned beam of sunlight illuminated the forehead of the idol of Ram Lalla, creating a radiant ray inside the sanctum. This unique ritual, achieved through a sophisticated system of mirrors and lenses, beautifully merges ancient tradition with modern science, symbolising divine blessings on the auspicious day marking the birth of Lord Ram.

title: Chaos in J&K Assembly as National Conference MLAs protest over Khamenei’s
author: ET Online
long_desc: Chaos broke out in the Jammu and Kashmir Assembly on Friday as the National Conference (NC) led protests over the killing of Iran’s former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and the ongoing US-Israeli military actions in Iran. Video footage from the House showed NC legislators raising slogans and holding posters and portraits of Khamenei, triggering sharp reactions from BJP members. The situation quickly escalated into chaos, with a scuffle breaking out between Congress MLA Irfan Hafiz Lone and BJP MLA Yudvir Sethi.

title: Karnataka HC orders notice on Lokayukta clean chit to CM Siddaramaiah, others in MUDA case
author: ET Bureau
short_desc: The Karnataka High Court has issued notices to the Lokayukta police, ED, and Chief Minister Siddaramaiah regarding a petition challenging the closure of a case on alleged unlawful plot allotment in Mysuru. An activist's plea contests the special court's acceptance of the Lokayukta's closure report,
long_desc: The Karnataka High Court on Thursday ordered notices to the Lokayukta police, the Enforcement Directorate (ED), and Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, among others, on a petition challenging the closure of the case related to alleged unlawful allotment of plots in Mysuru linked to Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, his wife, and two others.Justice S Sunil Dutt Yadav was hearing a plea filed by activist Snehamayi Krishna, which contested a Bengaluru special court’s decision to accept a closure report submitted by the Lokayukta Police in January this year. The special court’s order provided relief to Siddaramaiah and the other accused in a case that drew widespread attention in 2024.The special court, designated to hear cases involving elected representatives, had accepted the ‘B report’ filed by the…

title: Dhurandhar 2 fallout: Ex-IG EXPOSES Atiq Ahmed's Pak connection, fake currency network links
author: ET Online
long_desc: Former IG (Prayagraj) Surya Kumar recounted the rise of gangster Atiq Ahmed, revealing that Atiq was involved in instigating riots in Allahabad in 1986. When police cracked down, Atiq fled to Bombay and gradually became a mafia leader, acquiring disputed land cheaply and selling it for profit while threatening or killing opponents. Surya Kumar also disclosed that Atiq, along with Shahabuddin of Siwan and Mukhtar Ansari of Mau, had links to a group of ISI operatives from Pakistan who printed fake Indian currency, which was sent via the Pakistani embassy in Kathmandu. They used the money to expand their criminal networks and increase profits.
